Customer_Pain_Points:
In a real emergency, people panic and can't find what they need in a messy "bag of Band-Aids. When a child is screaming, they can't find the antiseptic wipes because they are buried under 50 loose Band-Aids.

Most consumer kits only treat "boo-boos" (minor scrapes), leaving the user helpless during serious accidents (deep cuts, sprains). "Fluff" items. They hate kits filled with 100 Q-tips and safety pins but zero
Splits, CPR Kits, or Zip Stitch Laceration Closures.

First aid kits often end up buried at the bottom of a trunk or back of a closet, making them useless when seconds count. 

The Value of Reliability: "I don't want to have to buy a cheap first aid kit twice or worry about it not being helpful in an emergency." Traditional "drugstore" kits are too fragile. They break open in a vibrating truck bed, and the contents are usually insufficient for high-energy trauma.

Many mass-market kits use "filler" tools—plastic tweezers that can't grip a splinter or "trauma shears" that struggle to cut through a simple seatbelt or thick denim.
Review_Highlights:
This SurviveX First Aid Kit is surprisingly well-organized and packed with quality supplies for home and travel. The labeled compartments make it easy to find what you need in a hurry. It's comprehensive, durable, and gives peace of mind, though you might not have much room to add extra items.  Full Review: I recently picked up the SurviveX Large First Aid Kit for car, travel, and home use, and I'm pretty happy with it so far. I needed a kit that could handle a wide variety of situations but wouldn't take up too much space in my car or backpack. Weekend trips are frequent, and I wanted the security of a well-stocked first aid kit. After comparing several options, SurviveX stood out due to its quality components, comprehensive contents, price, and HSA/FSA eligibility.  My first thought when I got the kit was, "Wow, this is well-thought-out!" The bag is divided into clearly labeled mesh pockets (wound dressings, eye pads, gloves, minor wounds, etc.). Everything is easy to see and grab – a critical feature when every second counts. The bag itself seems incredibly durable. It has multiple attachment points, so you can mount or carry it however is most convenient. I especially appreciate the strap system, which lets you unzip the bag and access everything while it's still mounted. That's a definite plus for accessibility.  The kit is 'very' full. While that's great, it doesn't leave much room to add extra items specific to your needs (EpiPen fits fine, mass trauma dressings not so much, for example). Whether that's a plus or a minus depends on if you're planning on adding more supplies and how much you need to add. I'll definitely be utilizing my leftover FSA funds to pick up more of these so I'll have a kit dedicated for home and in our vehicles. Overall, I'm extremely satisfied with this purchase. I wouldn't hesitate to recommend it to anyone looking for a high-quality, comprehensive first aid kit.

Granted, we can tell this has a lot of pieces to it.  But the thing I find most impressive about this first aid kit is that it appears to have been designed by someone who actually knew what they were doing, and not just someone trying to get the item count increased so they could claim more parts!  This feels like quality, the outer case itself as well as the items inside. It's packing a lot of potential medical assistance punch into a fairly small space!  As I said... some THOUGHT went into what to include and how to arrange things to make it actually useful and FINDABLE... One common problem with first aid kits is how "maybe it's here, but I can't find it!" happens...  In a panic or emergency situation, it can be overwhelming for someone not familiar with function and usability of all those little things... so this one LABELS the parts and their FUNCTIONALITY... i.e. what you need for cuts, scrapes, eye problems, breaks, ... whatever... that's pretty cool, and fairly rare.  Yes, the kit is more expensive than many of its competitive offerings... but USEFULNESS and FUNCTION and SAFE OUTCOME is the purpose of these things... not just price or quantity of useless pieces tossed into an unlabeled kit.  This kit has none of that uselessness.... It's all about USEFULNESS and GUIDANCE... truly a calming gift in an emergency.  I can't recommend it more highly!

Feels very high quality, good construction. Things are all labeled and color coded nicely. It's pretty packed so it'll be hard to add things inside if you wanted to, but theres some straps on the outside you could attach to. Hopefully I'll never really have to use it. Most annoying thing is that they didn't list exactly what comes with the kit in the item listing, but they do on the box it comes in. For now 4 stars, because I haven't used anything inside yet, if I do, i'll update the review, though hopefully I never actually have to use it

This compact kit contains a good variety of the things that you need short of hauling around a full trauma kit. The arrangement of the contents is well thought out and makes it easy to find what you need in short order. The compact size makes it easy to store in a vehicle, RV or travel trailer so you have it wherever you go. The quality of the outer container is good, no obvious shortcuts in the sewing/materials. Mine was missing the little Velcro flag shown in the listing, but that is not a major sticking point.

Seems to have everything one could need for common issues/ injuries and is easy to locate items with guide book. I ordered this mostly in case of a natural disaster but ended up utilizing it after slicing my finger with a knife (which, in hindsight, was obviously the more realistic scenario).

I'm excited to have a real first aid kit in our house. Previously, our idea of first aid preparedness was a box of bandaids, a tube of Neosporin and a random roll of adhesive tape left over from a 1996 injury. I was also excited to see all the extra pockets on the outside of the kit. Hopefully we'll never need to use it, but it's good to know we have the supplies if needed, and even better to know they are all in one, easy to grab and go place.

As a registered nurse who can appreciate having medical supplies on hand, this bag seems to check most of the boxes. It's organized well and has a nice pair of trauma shears that I wanted to remove from the bag and use at work. Everything is identified and there is a first aid booklet included that offers basic instructions. I recommend it as a nice addition to your emergency supplies. I think most of us doomsday preppers will appreciate it.

I was taken aback by the plethora of products to choose from when selecting a first aide package for my home. I was looking for more than a cut finger/ bee sting pack. I wanted a one time purchase that would support just about any anomaly that could be reasonably or even unreasonably expected. The SeviceX kit, I believe fulfills that need at a competitive price. One thing I spent a fair amount of time with was researching what actually is contained in the kit. So many kits have some big total items but extra aspirins won’t help if a serious wound is encountered. I am pleased after receiving the kit as the quality and inventory meet my expectations.

I purchased this kit as part of an emergency ready kit I have been building for severe weather/ natural disasters. I feel like if we ever needed an emergency survival kit the time is now due to all of the recent weather related things that has happened all over the u.s. I honestly thought it was going to have a few things I could add to my kit and then purchase more stuff separately but let me tell you this kit has everything you could possibly need in a emergency survival situation when it comes to first aid. If you are thinking of purchasing this product, go ahead and pull that trigger and make that purchase. You will not be disappointed!!

Got this to put in the car so I can be prepared for more than just scrapes. It’s smaller than I anticipated, which is nice (fits perfectly in one of my side trunk compartments in the Honda odyssey), and has everything packed tightly, neatly, and well labeled. There’s a lot in this pack. I ordered a couple of heavy duty tourniquets and were able to fit them and my son’s EpiPens on top. Impressed and pleased with this purchase!

I would say that this is a good kit for the price. Lots of good quality supplies. Plan on spending some time opening it up and unwrapping certain things but the good news is that all the bags inside are only ziplock sealed, not heat sealed so that don’t need to be cut or ripped open. There’s enough room inside for a few other things but generally there’s not much room for extra supplies. I also like that everything is clearly labeled which makes finding stuff in an emergency easier.

Great emergency kit! Most kits I get have mostly band aids and gauze but this kit has a little bit of everything. The cool part is it comes with a little book with sections for each type of wound. The book sections are color codes and correlate with the color codes on the sections inside the bag. It makes it very easy to locate specific items in the bag. The bag is a decent size and it will easily fit in the trunk of any small car. I couldn’t believe how much stuff could fit in that small bag and you would think it would be hard to utilize being so packed full but the way the bag is laid out. It’s very easy to use.

The "SurviveX Large First Aid Kit for Car, Travel & Home" is the ideal sized first aid kit, it’s also FSA HSA Approved. This kit fit perfectly in the frunk and attached exactly where I expected it on the Molle panels without the need for any additional straps, the quick release Velcro attachment is ingeniously designed when you are in an emergency situation and need to quickly get to someone's aid.

It's a really nice kit. Very organized and the little booklet for what to do is a nice addition. We bought 2 of these to go in each car. It is a bit large but that's okay as it's easy to spot if I need to ask someone to grab it out of the car. I threw a few extra band-aids of various sizes just in case. The back Molle attachment actually is a velcro panel that can be removed so if your car has a felt back, it can just stick to the back seats.

I bought this for my household to use for whatever life throws at us. Every thing was packed and labeled very nicely. There are two glow sticks in the pack that i missed in the description. Hubby was happy about that. The storage is amazing and the quality of the products are great. When it comes to safety it has products that are safe for me to use as an allergy suffer.

I’m putting together a go bag in case of emergencies and this first aid kit was an important addition. It’s a high-quality sturdy backpack and has all the major needs organized by section so it folds out in the middle. I think with this I have basically everything and just need to put our specific prescriptions in to make sure that we have our bases covered. I highly recommend this first aid kit. It’s much better than the other I’d ordered that did not have nearly enough. So it’s a good value and also takes FSA funds.

I have bought a number of these over the years. This is by far the best. Great pockets and access. Plus it is a LABELED! My wife is nurse and even she was impressed.

Kit was much nicer than I expected as I compared pricing to other similar products on the market. The carrying case is very nice and the products inside are very well organized. Hopefully I’ll never need to use it but I feel confident it was what I need if I find myself in a situation needing it. Gives me peace of mind!

The kit contains a wide variety of first aid needs. It's packaged in a conveniently size case. The material is sturdy and the stitching is tight. It appears that it will hold up for a long time.

Solid overall first aid kit. Has plenty of options for covering a wide range of situations. Would love to see the option to purchase refill kits or individual items for replacement when used.

I'm accident prone. My previous kit died (it was a steralite one my mom gave me) from being knocked around in the cold Michigan winters. Soft sided, completely packed.

In a real emergency, people panic and can't find what they need in a messy "bag of Band-Aids. When a child is screaming, they can't find the antiseptic wipes because they are buried under 50 loose Band-Aids.

Most consumer kits only treat "boo-boos" (minor scrapes), leaving the user helpless during serious accidents (deep cuts, sprains). "Fluff" items. They hate kits filled with 100 Q-tips and safety pins but zero
Splits, CPR Kits, or Zip Stitch Laceration Closures.

First aid kits often end up buried at the bottom of a trunk or back of a closet, making them useless when seconds count.

Many mass-market kits use "filler" tools—plastic tweezers that can't grip a splinter or "trauma shears" that struggle to cut through a simple seatbelt or thick denim.
Review_Highlights:
It comes pretty well stuffed, but everything is well organized, even the pockets are labeled. A good bit of thought went into designing this first aid kit. It arrived with everything in the bag except for a package of large bandages. I like the fold out design of the bag, as it makes most items easy to access in spite of it being stuffed full. I did check and and it appears SurviveX does replacements for most of the consumable items in this kit. Of course a first aid instructional booklet is also included. The bag looks and feels to be of good quality. There's a shoulder strap included for easier carrying. There's front zipper section on the bag that's empty, but due to how packet the bag is, it's hard to fit anything in it other than say a piece of paper. I would have liked to see an expiration date on the included glow sticks, as they do have a limited shelf life. It also would have been nice to have a packing list included with item numbers for easy ordering of replacement consumables. The only things that are missing are some basic pain reliever medications and a tourniquet. At this price point, I would have liked to see a good tourniquet included. Overall, it's quite an extensive kit to the point of it being way more than an individual will likely ever need. Feels geared towards someone or a group being away from civilization for a while or someone designated to provide first-aid at a public event.

This is a well organized and thought out emergency preparations essential bag for layman or professional to have on hand for those unexpected emergencies. I am an Emergency and Trauma trained licensed health professional and feel that this is a good first aid kit to gift to family, friends but also for businesses to have on hand for emergencies. They have packed a lot of supplies into this kit. The organization of the bag is one thing that I truly appreciate. Even if the mesh gets stretched as it will with time and use; the bags housing the items will still keep everything organized. The trauma scissors are good and sharp and will cut through jeans. The tweezers are appropriate for smaller needs. Having the compartments clearly label aides in identifying which item is needed. I did find that knowing where the fever scan strips, tweezers and shears are located helps as they are slightly hidden. I don’t need the first aid guide but will keep it in the kit as someone might need it to give me aid. The CPR kit with instructions for adult and children being detachable is helpful along with the instruction reminder on the flap but keep in mind that guidelines do change. The bag holding all the first aid is nylon so it will wipe clean easy. The zippers are dual for faster access to the contents and the zippers opened and closed smoothly. There is an exterior front zipper area but it’s tight due to the kits contents; I might be able to fit a small pad of paper, pin light into that pocket. There is an exterior side pocket that stretches and holds the 6 inch bandage. There is also a shallow zipper pocket just above the region of the shears/tweezers which could hold other items such as a tourniquet or hemostats. The grip handles are comfortable and there is an included strap for carrying. I feel a pen or marker is better than a pencil but that’s my preference for body marking for redness, swelling and more. The straps on the back make it easy to tuck my stethoscope into the kit. The thing I didn’t like is the Blue wound tape was missing from the kit. Minor things but not deal breaker are the following. The alcohol prep pad and the antiseptic towelettes being the same white and blue color is easy to grab the wrong item when in a hurry: it would be nice if one was red and the other blue for a more rapid identification. The bandage strips are not identifiable by the package so you need to hold it up to a light to see which bandage is in the wrapping.

This LARGE first aid kit is a monster. A huge supply of almost anything you could ever need short of surgery. It covers wound care, personal items and has a variety of basic tools including trauma shears, gloves, pins to dig our splinters. It has a note pad, blanket - just about everything you could possibly need for basic first aid - all neatly organized and individually wrapped. Makes a great family first aid kit for camping - keeping near the back door for family outings, or in your emergency supplies for weather disasters. Impressive kit and handy to keep around.

HIGHLY impressed with this. Nice RED kit. You always know it's a first aid kit. It's got a nice MOLLE attachment straps on the back to easily put this on the back of a Tactical vest, on a tactical bag, etc. I'd say this is great to throw inside another bag, but this is big. It may fit in another bag, but this thing was designed to be easily accessible. Once you open this bag, there is a shoulder strap that you can use to carry this from a long strap instead of mounting this to a MOLLE mount. This does have velcro on the front as well, so you can attach a patch, so you can put your american flag on there, or some other funny patch like the "Ouch Pouch" Open this up and it's very well organized and very well stocked. It's clearly marked for what you need and are looking for. Something most people dont think about is what happens if someone gets hurt at the dark of night? This thing has 2 glow stick in there so you have some light. There is stuff in here for major wounds to minor cuts. Even has some burn gel and cooling pack. There are foam bandages and compression bandages. It even comes with a first aid guide manual with instructions and pictures of what to do for basic first aid. Of all things in this pack, i'm not seeing any bleed stop granules. :( This is a really good kit. I will be keeping this in my truck so wherever i go, i'll have this close by. Would i recommend this? Absolutely. This is one of the best first aid kits i've seen.

This SurviveX first aid kit has everything you need to deal with on the ground emergencies. I was truly impressed with the range of items in the pack and how everything was labeled so you can find what you are looking for at a glance. Thoughtful additions you won't find in most kits are glow sticks, emergency whistle, burn dressings, tourniquet, space blanlet, cold packs and a large conforming splint. There are also several zippered pockets where you can stash additional items that might be needed more frequently. The back of the pack has a sticky velcro full length patch that allows you to attach it to your exisiting backpack for backcountry usage. The quality of all the items is exemplary. As the designated safety officer for a kids camp I feel assured this kit will assist me with any emergency I might encounter.

The Large Pro kit arrived in a branded box that was well labeled (the contents are listed on an end). Inside the box was the red bag and the emergency bandage (hemorrhage control bandage) which didn’t fit into the bag. The flag on the outside has Velcro on the back, so it’s easily removable (or replaceable with a Velcro patch of your choosing since there’s a large area of Velcro on the bag). There are two outside pockets. The zippered front one won’t hold much with the bag full, but the side one is roomier depending on how tight you have the outside strap. There are multiple ways to attach this bag (flexibility!) and there’s a handle on one side. The two zippers that open the bag have pulls, look sturdy, and they glided open and close quickly and easily. Each section is color coded (as an educator I love this) and the overall organization of the pack is amazing. All the pockets are clearly labeled. The thoughtfulness that went into designing this is obvious. Everything has a place. Almost all items are branded SurviveX, and the packets are easy tear with instructions on one side (always helpful) and often a clear window so you can see what’s inside. The ones that should be resealable are resealable, and the packets that should be sterile are marked sterile. Most items have expiration dates (I saw 2030-06, 2030-07, and 2028-06 with a quick check). A well-organized, color-coded, and easy-to-read first aid booklet is included, and it sits perfectly in its labeled pocket). There’s even a resealable pouch for a few of your personal meds. Add a few of these and you’re set. Most items state “Responsibly made in China” and “Designed with care in Virginia”.

This is a comprehensive, impressive first aid/go-bag/survival-emergency kit. For hiking, camping or even just to store in your vehicle, this is a must-have. The contents cover all types of injuries, from minor cuts, to trauma wounds, to joint injuries to CPR. In addition, there is an emergency blanket for warmth and glow sticks for light. The bag itself has a number of additional compartments and pouches on the outside for adding any additional items you may need, such as medications or necessary personal items in case an injury or emergency leaves you stranded for a time. The only thing missing would be perhaps MREs or emergency food bars. You might also want to tuck in a water purification device if you have one. When I first removed this from the box, I was wondering why they called this "large." Once you unzip it and start looking through compartments, it becomes plain that the contents are plentiful, but the package itself is smartly-designed to be compact. In one of the pictures, you'll see a pen perched on top of the kit for scale. In another, I've laid a standard composition book next to the kit, again, for scale. If you have kids who like to hike or camp, I'd get them each one. But, that's just the Mom in me. I think these would be good Christmas presents for people who enjoy the outdoors or otherwise like to look for adventure.

Wow, this is a truly impressive large first aid kit. It's about the size of a shoebox. It has lots of MOLLE attachment points, including a panel on the back so it can be attached to a MOLLE harness and ripped off in an emergency. I don't see how it can't have everything you could possibly need. I do a lot of construction and also camping, so I plan to keep this in my truck. It will be fine for emergencies, even if several people are injured. My favorite feature of this kit is that everything has it's own little labeled home. It's so nice not having to sort through a bunch of similar looking packages trying to find what you need. So that's a huge plus to me. I also like how it folds open after unzipping. Plus, you can buy most of the supplies from the same company if you use some. I cut off the side of the box that has the content list and put it in the bag so I have a quick reference as to what's inside. Currently listed at $121, I think it's a great value. It's perfect for keeping in a bunker, camper, job site, etc. The bottle in the pictures is 16oz for scale.

This FAK may be expensive but it is DEFINITELY worth the price. You get some really good quality medical supplies in this. If you're looking for a first aid kit for small cuts and scrapes, just get regular bandages. This has some trauma gear in it. Everything is really nicely packaged and vacuum sealed. They have an included Isreali bandage and it's sterile! I didn't even think they would also add 2 glow sticks. Granted I think they're already broken because I can hear broken shards shaking inside it. The red bag is very good quality. It has a molle webbing in the back and it's connected with a velcro. If you have a surface area in your car/truck that has velcro attached, you can quick attach this to your car. The included shears are also great. It has so many wonderful medical supplies but I'll keep these for when I go camping or in a big emergency. Once you use the medical supplies, which is 100% labeled and really well labeled in each pocket/compartment, you can definitely use this bag for other stuff too.

My buddies and I enjoy off road adventures. But out in the wilderness, there's no immediate help to rely on in case of an emergency. It is important to be able to address illness or mechanical injury. For years, I've kept the grizzly adventure first aid kit in my truck. What did not come with that kit, I supplemented. This kit actually comes with the items that I added to that kit. And more. Though there is one item that is not included here, and that is a packet of quick clot. Most if the items a secured, but there are a few open pockets and I am not of fan of that. In a hurry, many of those tiny items will go flying about. So you end up losing the benefits of having an organized kit. There is also a tourniquet included that does not kit on the inside of the kit. Fortunately, the minor negatives don't weigh much against the positives.

This large pro first aid kit is really comprehensive! Perfect size for car, travel, and home use with enough supplies for various emergencies. The emergency kit covers all the basics plus extra. Including zip stitch wound closure strips is impressive! These are professional-grade wound closure tools that can really help in serious situations. Much better than basic first aid kits. Perfect for hiking, camping, backpacking, and outdoor adventures where you might need serious medical supplies. Having everything organized and ready gives peace of mind. The kit is well-stocked with bandages, gauze, tape, antiseptic, and all the essentials you'd need for emergencies. The zip stitch strips set it apart from basic kits. Being large enough for comprehensive care but still portable for travel is perfect. Fits in the car, RV, or carries easily for outdoor activities. Everything is organized and accessible. Really happy with this first aid kit! Comprehensive supplies, includes professional zip stitch strips, and perfect for car, travel, and outdoor use. Great for anyone wanting serious emergency preparedness beyond basic first aid!

Wow, so happy with this SurviveX Large Pro First Aid Kit!. Way bigger than other kits I had before and comes with everything you need for emergencies, at home or car or like for the outdoors. The color-coded compartments makes finding stuff super fast when ur stressed, no more digging around. It’s got these zip wound things that close cuts easy, like magic, no need for stitches! So clever, I never saw it in any other kit. The bag is tough also and it fits well in my car, plus you can mount it on backpacks which is awesome for hiking trips. I like that it's made by a small business too. Really hope I won’t need it for anything serious but it gives me and my family peace of mind for sure. Definately recommend to anyone who wants to be ready for anything. 5 stars all the way!

First, let me say that I've done hands on first aid for 6 years for quite a few different injuries and put two of my own comprehensive first aid kits together. This first aid kit is $200 at the time of this review, which is certainly not cheap but it's an almost perfect kit for a car or base camp - somewhere you will rarely need it but will be really glad to have it in quite a few situations. I can also tell you from experience that you'll struggle to put it together for any less. This kit covers typical first aid but extends to tick bites, sprains, burns, etc. Included stuff seems to be decent quality, it's in resealable pouches and it's very well labelled and organized. Really important for quick access. There is also a nice guide with pictures to help you handle various situations. The pouch is good quality with molle attachment, shoulder strap and carry handle. And, crucially, has space for additional items and molle on the front for expansion.

This is a relatively -very- large first aid kit. It has most of what you'd need. I'll talk about the negative first due to it being just the price. The price at the time of writing this is almost $220, whereas the price at time of ordering was almost $30 less. When I ordered this, it was worth the price as it was a great deal. Now with the price increase, it's bordering a price that you could just assemble yourself. Any further increases and it's likely cheaper to just make it yourself. Now for the pros. First, it has a ton. Lots in there. It's all colour-coded to help you during an emergency. Anything to resolve normal wounds is in red packaging. Anything to deal with infections such as cleaning said wounds is in blue packaging. Things for hygiene and comfort are in teal, and some general tools are in black. The two blues have some crossover but ultimately this is okay. This pack includes dressings, sanitizers, bandaging, scissors, a blanket, hydrogels, gauze, zip closures, a splint, gloves, wipes, freeze packs, balms, creams, and a bag for sharps, among other things.

The biggest thing is the organization. In an emergency, you don't want to be fumbling, and the color-coded, labeled compartments (like one for "Wounds," one for "Tools," etc.) are brilliant. Everything is visible and easy to grab in a hurry. What makes this professional-grade is the contents. It goes way beyond minor cuts - there are supplies for sprains, burns, and severe bleeding. The feature I hope I never have to use but am glad I have is the emergency laceration closure. That's a true lifesaver for deep cuts where you can't get to a doctor right away, and the fact that it minimizes scarring is a huge bonus. It feels like having a secret weapon. The MOLLE compatibility is perfect. I have it strapped securely to the back of my hiking backpack, so it's always ready to go. The whole thing is durable, bright red, and easy to spot. Knowing it's FSA/HSA eligible made the purchase a total no-brainer. It’s expensive compared to a small home kit, but for anyone who camps, hikes, or just wants real peace of mind in the car, this is the comprehensive system you need.

This lunch bag sized wonder is fully packed and labeled with everything you need from glow sticks to an Israeli bandage. The bag can be connected to a sturdy object and ripped off thanks to its quick release Velcro backing. From there the items are nearly endless. My only mention is that there is no tourniquet. I feel like they opted out for the Israeli bandage since a tourniquet does need some training to use. This is why I feel so it isn’t a true loss overall but still mentionable. The included first aid manual is accurate and quite detailed.

What immediately stood out was the organization, unlike cheaper kits where supplies are just thrown in randomly, this has designated compartments with clear labeling that actually helps you find what you need quickly. The mesh pockets keep items visible and accessible, though some smaller tools like the fever strips and tweezers are tucked in spots that take a minute to locate initially, but once you know the layout it's fine. The trauma shears are high quality, I tested them on thick denim fabric and they cut cleanly without struggling, way better than the flimsy scissors most kits include. The bag material is nylon which wipes clean easily and feels durable enough for regular use. Dual zippers on the main compartment allow quick access and they glide smoothly without catching. The detachable CPR kit with instructions is a smart design choice, and having both adult and child guidelines right there is helpful.

I'm blown away by how good a deal this first aid kit is. It might seem a lot but when you look inside at how much you get, and how much they cost separately from SurviveX, this is a real steal. I've been really happy with the quality from SurviveX products for awhile, a lot of them are in this kit and they work when you need it to. This makes a great home kit, and I'm planning on getting another for my car later on. It even has zippered pockets for filling up with your favourite one use bandages, I'm personally going to put a bunch of Elastoplast bandages for minor cuts and scrapes to avoid using the premium stuff in this kit right away. I recommend it for sure, it even comes with a little CPR pouch with a valve and mask. They really thought of everything!

This is a large kit that has everything you need for your camping and hunting or at the house for the whole family or group. This is a large bag and definitely recommend using with a vehicle of some sort. This is also a good size to have in a workshop as there are plenty of Band-Aids, gauze and other large bandages, including Israeli and triangle bandages. There are are gloves and breathing mask protectors for CPR all contained in convenient and well marked pockets. The bag secures well with the zipper and fold out designs and has a Velcro mounting plate that uses Molle style fasteners so it can be hard mounted to something and removed quickly by peeling the bag off the mounting plate.

I have basic first aid and CPR training, so my perspective isn’t from a paramedic or medical professional—just from a mom, aunt, and someone who works at a school who’s dealt with everything from tiny scrapes to bigger situations. Over the years, I’ve tried to piece together my own comprehensive kits, but honestly, this one is well worth the investment. The quality of both the contents and the case itself is fantastic. It’s durable, thoughtfully designed, and has multiple ways to secure or mount it, whether in a vehicle, at home, or on a backpack. The organization is what really stands out—everything is colour-coded and clearly labeled by function, which makes it incredibly easy to find what you need in a stressful situation. Even someone without formal first aid training would find it intuitive and reassuring to use. It’s clear that a lot of thought went into designing this kit. The inclusion of wound closures and more advanced supplies really sets it apart from standard first aid kits. I genuinely wish the kits at my school were this well laid out. I’m very impressed—it’s one of those orders that gives real peace of mind knowing it’s ready when you need it.

HIGHLY impressed with this. Nice RED kit. You always know it's a first aid kit. It's got a nice MOLLE attachment straps on the back to easily put this on the back of a Tactical vest, on a tactical bag, etc. I'd say this is great to throw inside another bag, but this is big. It may fit in another bag, but this thing was designed to be easily accessible. Once you open this bag, there is a shoulder strap that you can use to carry this from a long strap instead of mounting this to a MOLLE mount. This does have Molle cuts on the front as well, so you can attach other Molle things to this and keep them close. Open this up and it's very well organized and very well stocked. It's clearly marked for what you need and are looking for. Something most people dont think about is what happens if someone gets hurt at the dark of night? This thing has 2 glow stick in there so you have some light. There is stuff in here for major wounds to minor cuts. Even has some burn gel and cooling pack. There are foam bandages and compression bandages. It even comes with a first aid guide manual with instructions and pictures of what to do for basic first aid. Of all things in this pack, i'm not seeing any bleed stop granules. :( This is a really good kit. I will be keeping this in my 4Runner so wherever my wife goes, she'll have this close by. Would i recommend this? Absolutely. This is one of the best first aid kits i've seen. 1 thing about this. This says waterproof. So this would be good to take on your boat, kayak, canoe, or whatever else on the water. Just beware.... the "zipper" is super hard to Lock closed and to open it up. I guess that's because of the waterproofing, it has to be extremely tight, but i feel like i'm going to rip the zipper off trying to close this.

I purchased this to keep in a box on my boat and I’m very pleased. It’s heavy duty, waterproof, and has a broad selection of well-labeled first-aid supplies.

Bought this for the wife for her jeep, she is a nurse and was very impressed with it and love how organized it was and the amount of helpfull first aid products that was in it.

When I chose this First Aid kit from SurviveX, I noticed it was waterproof. I didn’t think much more about it until the kit arrived. The design of the bag is very impressive. I feel like I could be on a river raft and lose this kit, find it down stream and still have dry first aid supplies. I have sone of the zip tie style wound kits from this company and have found them to be a fantastic option since we don’t live near a town with a hospital. Stitches can take a hour drive each way. The zip tie kit from SurviveX included in this kit, has solved that problem for my husband in less than 5 minutes. The exterior is a rubbery surface that gives the bag grip and the waterproofing mentioned above. The zippers are tight and move smoothly. The zippers are also waterproof so they are different from a typical plastic toothed zipper. There is even a pocket on the front with nothing in it that the end of the zipper tucks into a pocket to give the area a water tight seal. Someone wanting a compact, water proof and well designed kit, I think this is a great value.

Nice First aid kit it's in a semi hard waterproof case. As you can see, on the product page, it comes with ALMOST everything you may need for an emergencies. But missing some things I would feel necessary to make a FULL first aid kit (depending on the situation of course). Items that I feel that should be included are Pain meds, nausea, vaseline, etc. If you can fit them in. The case is pretty packed, and not much room to fit anything else, but you can fit a few pain meds etc in the case. The bag has compartment labels for everything that is included (Cooling Patch, Cuts & Scrapes, first aid Booklet, pencil, etc).

SurviveX makes a great first aid kit. We have a different larger kit from them and thought we could make good use of this one on camping trips or when we're on the water. Opening it, it hardly looks like the zipper has a seam. That's good for the water proof claim. Their video says it's IPX7 which means it can be submerged to 1 meter for 30 minutes without permanent damage. Checking the zippers, if you have it even unzipped a little, water is going to get it, but what might help with that claim is that the contents and bag won't be damaged. Just about everything in the kit is already in its own sealed bag. Bottom line, even if water does get in to the bag, the contents are going to be okay. If you took it kayaking, it's not going to be a big deal if it's at the back of the kayak and gets water on it or sits in a puddle.

This large waterproof case first aid kit is a wonderful addition to your car supplies. Perfectly trunk sized and bright colored waterproof case. Comes complete with almost any supply you could possibly need short of surgery. Instructions and supplies like gloves and trauma scissors included, even glow sticks. Nicely organized - this kit even includes the new zip stitch bandages - which are great for closing gaping wounds without stitches. A perfect temporary solution for emergencies. The case has traditional molle attachments and is a nice semi hard material, so it's flexible - yet sturdy and easy to lash to a backpack or gear.

Two weeks ago, my husband and I were on a camping trip. He fell while hiking on a mountain trail and broke a bone. We were completely unprepared for an emergency. We made it out, but not without a lot of trouble and more pain than was necessary. It would have been beneficial for us to have a first aid kit of this magnitude with us. The splint and pressure bandage, in particular, would have been very useful. This SurviveX first aid kit includes a lot of the more expensive specialized items that the other kit did not include. This first aid kit is designed to handle anything from minor boo boos to serious injuries, whereas the cheaper kit is designed for everyday minor injuries. I love that the bag is waterproof to protect all the supplies inside from the elements, which is important when camping and hiking. Every pouch is clearly labeled with the contents. The first aid supplies are in sealed packets to keep them sterile until use and most packets have a zip top closure so they can be resealed.

The little backpack style case is super sturdy and overall very well made. I would trust having it out in heavy rain and all the stuff inside remaining dry. I agree with another reviewer that the zippers are tough to open or close all the way (if you don't do the latter, well, there it goes the waterproofness). So being on your own with an injured arm/hand and trying to open it, could be a challenge for some folks. I think my favorite is the degree of organization. It covers pretty much any "standard" injury or "body trouble" (like overheating etc) you might encounter. It is super compact and pretty lightweight which makes it easy to store or travel around with.

Overall this kit is quite comprehensive in its contents and it's organized and labeled to such a degree that even if it was your first time opening it while in the confusion of needing to treat someone you could find what you're looking for. The bag is very high quality similar to something that you'd see in a military kit with Molle webbing on the front to attach additional bags if required and a panel on the back which has the Molle attachments as well. The bag has really high quality water resistant zippers that easily open and close because of the large t-shaped zipper handles and the bag is coated in a water resistant coating allowing it to resist sprays or potentially rain if you were to be bringing this on camping or outdoorsing trips.

As a camper, fisherman, outdoorsman, and medic, and parent, I've owned a lot of first aid kits over the years. This SurviveX kit is impressive. Of course it has all the basic bandages, gauze, etc. But it also has a ton of 'second tier' stuff like a CPR barrier mask, glow sticks, a thermal blanket, trauma shears, and a limb splint. Most of these things I usually have to add myself. The bag is not a flimsy, floppy bag either. It's a semi-rigid box/bag, with a robust, waterproof zipper. It has a velcro mounting panel on the back with molle straps that allows the bag to be easily torn away from its mounting pad when you need to grab it in an emergency.

I really like this first aid kit. The bag itself seems of good quality and sturdy and the size is compact. It seems just right for something to keep around in ones car (should travel well slipped under a seat or in the back), at work or at home. There's most everything you'd need, bandages, various dressings, gloves, first aid booklet, trauma shears..etc. And even things you wouldn't necessarily think of of packing, like personal care (includes floss and face masks), instant cold packs, trauma shears, cooling patches, glow sticks, and even a cpr kit with basic directions shown when you open the little pocket.

I've picked up several first aid kits from Amazon for boating and travel, but this SurviveX large waterproof one is easily the nicest. The case feels solid—thick, durable material with waterproof zippers that seal tight, and big handles make it easy to pull open in emergencies. I like the MOLLE attachments for hooking it onto a backpack and the sturdy carrying handles. What really stands out is how everything inside is sorted into clear, labeled sections, so you can find what you need fast in a panics. It's loaded with way more than the basics- like zip stitches, safety pins, an emergency blanket, and glow sticks. Tons of stuff overall, and it's ideal for the boat, camping, or any outdoor trip where you want to be prepared.

Overall, this is a solid, well-organized first aid kit - probably one of the best ready-to-go options on the market. However, it does not include everything I need, and it is packed so tightly that there's hardly any room for additional items. So for me it's rather a TRAUMA kit to stabilize someone before medical help arrives, rather than a comprehensive first aid kit. For that reason I have this kit in my car, but made a custom-build first aid kit for home - with much more medications. Having said that, I personally added an emergency "israeli" bandage permanently and usually add a travel medicine kit whenever I travel far. Both from the same company, SurviveX.

Wow, the container/bag alone is incredible. The zipper and rubber covering are serious, like take this thing whitewater rafting and don't worry about it, serious. The packing and contents are very well thought out and even the included first aid booklet is well done. It doesn't do much good to have a great first aid kit but no one knows how to treat injuries (or the medic is the one that gets hurt). The case even has molle straps or a full velcro back to give mounting options. Definitely not heading into the wilderness without this.

This kit truly has everything you'd need for common emergencies, according to my two practical nurse friends who checked it out with me! Each compartment has a tag that labels what goes in it and explains when and how to use them. It's like having a mini first aid guide built right into your supplies. Perfect for people like me who might panic in an emergency and forget basic procedures. The container is well built, and it zips tight, with the zipper going in far enough to create a water-resistant seal on it.

I use the small kit as my motorcycle medpack, and I have carried it on my bike every day since buying it. Recently, I was hit by a metal ramp that flew off the back of a truck and crashed hard. My kit hit the pavement at 80 and the supplies scattered on the freeway. Despite the high adrenaline situation, I was able to find my pressure bandage in the wreckage, stop the bleeding, and splint my leg. This kit is well organized and the supplies are very high quality. Personally, I would recommend attaching a high quality TQ to the exterior molle, and replacing the booklet with a Sam Splint. Overall, this is a great product, and Is the only kit I would buy from Amazon.

Nice Road Trip kit. Everything is well-marked, and well-organized. The only thing missing (in my opinion) is a couple packets of Bleed Stop, which I added. I'm pleased with the kit, and I do recommend it. This one is a keeper.

Good general first aid kit. I was first attracted by the photo that shows the organization for a quick find of what you need but the kit seems crowded and not as easy to find as expected without pulling things apart. I've had other kits where it really was quick and easy to find what you needed in an emergency. The kit fits in the glove box of my Ford F-150 so the size is convenient.

This is one of the more complete better organized first aid kits. The scissors actually work . It includes a thermometer and tweezers for splinters. Small but mighty. A necessity for the inevitable cuts and scrapes.

This SurviveX is the best first aid kit out there due to its complete-ness. I overland/off-road with my 5 year old, falling and tumbling are necessary part of the adventure. This kit came in so handy for large knee scrapes, cuts, and more. The adhesive is super strong but doesn't rip off skin like my previous kit which i love. Spend the extra money, you won't regret it!

I purchased this for my hiking trip to Patagonia, I love how it comes very organized and love how it is labelled. I appreciate how the kit still has space for some of the things I added in it. I highly recommend this product when you are going for a hike, camping or to just put in your car.

I guess it’s my own fault for not checking the dimensions, but I wouldn’t exactly consider this a ‘size small’. It’s more of a thick medium. The little backpack is very well made, compact, and rugged. There’s a Velcro strip on the front with a little removable USA flag. That’s cool. The silver strip below it that says ‘First Aid Kit’ is reflective, also. I love that everything inside is clearly labeled and organized to a T. Scratches an itch in my little OCD brain. I wouldn’t recommend using it for hiking/backpacking just due to the bulk. It would take up too much room, imho. Personally, I got this for my husband‘s work backpack. Sadly, this is too big for his backpack so it will be going in the car for emergencies.

The emergency kit's red exterior is a thoughtful choice, enhancing visibility in trunks or bags. However, the internal color scheme could be improved: - Many internal items are also red, including the tape spool, whistle, CPR mini-bag, and some plastic packets. This makes it challenging to quickly locate some items. - The print in the emergency manual is small, and folks with vision problems may have trouble reading it. - There's a fair amount of layering so you may need to thumb through items to find what you seeking. - Lack of a designated spot for common medications like ibuprofen or aspirin is an oversight.

At first I wasn't impressed because I couldn't find an inventory list and each bag was only minimally labeled with the contents. But the inventory changes every time you use anything so the list only helps when deciding to buy the kit. And each bag has a really logical selection of items. The CPR kit has a list of reminders sewn in and an extra set of gloves tucked in there. The trauma shears are extremely easy to grab. The only thing you should note is that there are no medications included. In the section for personal care there is a bunch of tiny plastic baggies for you to put your personal medications in.

This kit is very neat and organized and the selection of first aid items is well thought out. It's also pretty complete for dealing with minor medical issues. I have added a tube of antibiotic ointment as well as some aspirin, ibuprofen, and an antihistamine. There's a perfect empty spot to include a tube of ointment and they thoughtfully included a small personal meds pouch with little ziploc bags for you to keep pills organized in. The pouch itself is bright red, thick and well made with a smooth zipper. Inner mesh pouches are labelled logically and keeping this kit organized will be pretty much automatic.

First off, I was blown away at the organization of this IFAK(individual first aid kit) and I will be incorporating it into my primary bug-out bag as well as buying a few more for my car and family. Everything in the pack is organized cleanly and with clear notation as to what goes where. All items that could be affected by water are sealed in little packs that also allow them to be resealed. The included CPR kit is my favorite part, as it not only includes the CPR mouthpiece itself but a set of gloves, and some sewn-in instructions for CPR for not only adults but children as well.

The carrying case has multiple straps that make it easier to carry. For example, it can be connected to a backpack very easily. The inside of the case is better organized, with multiple separate compartments and printed labels describing major injury types. In comparison, with the cheaper cases I own, the first aid materials tend to be packed haphazardly into every nook and corner, making it difficult to find any single item in a hurry. This pack includes a couple of zip stitches, which I haven't received in any other kit I've ordered in the past.

The kit is well organized with specifically labeled pockets and colour coded packages that indicate normal use scenarios. The bag itself is high quality with premium zippers, durable material, and straps with buttons to attach the kit to another object or bag. The kit also includes zip wound closures along with many other high quality basic first aid supplies and has space so you can compliment it with more supplies. It opens like a book, rather than top to bottom like some kits do. The mounting options are also great, with the straps on the back.

Review_Highlights:
Its a real kit for real wounds! My husband is clumsy, so I got three, just in case. It has everything you need inside the sealed bag. Its sterile so don't open it until needed.

Excellent quality “substitute stitches” for cuts! With four boys at home, we love to keep these on hand. Excellent quality!

Most households have a basic first-aid kit for the occasional finger nicked from chopping vegetables, or a scraped knee when your child falls off their bike. But what do you do when someone receives a deeper, or wider cut that is bleeding profusely? How do you bandage the injury quickly and minimize the bleeding, so you can get the injured person to your doctor or hospital as fast as possible, and avoid having to call an ambulance? Or, maybe you've been hiking, you've fallen, and no ambulance can easily reach you for a while. What do you do? This wound closure kit comes in a tear-open pouch (that can be resealed, like a Zip-loc bag). The medical supplies inside are geared towards injuries that may be slightly more serious than the kind most basic first-aid kits are equipped to handle. It includes wound dressing pads, a roll of gauze, cloth tape, antiseptic & alcohol wipes, 2 pairs of nitrile gloves, and a safety scissors for easy cutting of the cloth tape. There's even a large transparent dressing that is waterproof, to better protect the wound from becoming infected in the first place. But the BEST thing in this kit is what they describe as a "Two-Way Zip Wound Closure." It's a butterfly-type adhesive pad that adheres to opposite sides of a wound, which can then be pulled together by a zip-tie system, squeezing the laceration shut. This helps to stanch excess bleeding. FOUR of these clamps are provided, so multiple small injuries can be handled with this kit. Or, a single, larger wound can be closed by applying several zip wound closures to the injured area. It might be somewhat difficult for people NOT intimately familiar with all the tools & supplies in this upgraded medkit, to get up to speed quickly in an emergency. Fortunately, a nicely laminated instruction card is included in the pouch, with helpful tips for you to read. The instruction card contains some information in a simple, digestible form, and it's no trouble to keep it in the pouch with everything else.

We've been marvelously free from lacerations since acquiring the SurviveX Zip Stitch Wound Closure Strips Kit, but my spouse is a nurse and says the strips are good quality and will be great for camping, and that the kit itself has a good balance of items: wipes for cleaning the damaged skin for application, sterile gloves, wound dressings, and also the actual strips that will hold wounds together. With spousal guidance, this was pretty easy to use, but I would recommend that people with no formal first aid training walk through the process (included on a little card) with one of the dressings and strips, as there are a lot of pieces and the order in which they are used is important.

Between running the ranch, playing in my woodshop, working on my truck, and my honey-do list, I’m frequently finding ways to get wounded. Usually this occurs a few times a week (sorry to be gross). So, we invested in this SurviveX Zip Stitch Wound Closure Strips kit. I thought I’d better open it up and familiarize myself with the contents, as well as how to use it. It comes in one of those durable type zip-lock bags. There’s quite a bunch of stuff in there (se pic), but there’s only 4 of the actual Zip Stitches. If they’re just like stitches, I’m guessing that all 4 could, in theory, be used up on one wound. This could make the kit a bit costly, however, much less costly than a trip to the hospital. There are also several wounds worth of dry bandages and tape for those lesser wounds that don’t require stitches.

I like that this kit contains everything you would need if you find yourself somewhere without being able to get help. This kit has been helpful to carry on hikes in the mountains. It is very easy to slip and cut your hands and legs while hiking. I have already used this kit once to help another hiker with a cut from a fall. The materials worked great. The zip stitch wound enclosure strips really did a great job of holding the wound closed. In this waterproof bag, you will receive the following items. One laminated set of instructions, two pairs of nitrile gloves, one medical scissors, six alcohol prep pads, four zip stitch wound enclosures, two non-adherent wound dressings squares (large and small), two rolls of gauze, one roll of breathable cloth tape, and one large transparent dressing square.

I am really impressed by this wound first aid kit! I was expecting to refill my supplies and instead found this to be a complete kit. I won't repeat details from the listing but suffice to say this should have what you need to handle moderate wounds. Unlike some kits, this one has a nice supply of each type item. It even comes with a laminated instruction sheet that already has a hole and grommet for hanging. The durable, waterproof bag holds things together in a small enough package this kit could easily stay in your car.

This is a decent wound closure kit. This is one of those supplies that I'd recommend adding to a well-established emergency trauma kit. It provides trauma shears, wipes, basic dressing, gloves, and non-invasive wound closures which utilize zip ties. This is great for emergency preparedness in general, but especially if you go camping or live in a more remote, rural location. It's in no way a replacement for a trip to the doctor or hospital. However, it can help close a wound in an emergency situation until you're able to get to a hospital for professional treatment. The adhesive is strong and will hold the skin together in place.

Perfect for tossing in a day pack or even leaving in the car for emergencies. Comes with gloves, tape, gauze, dressings, shears, and of course the zip closures and instructions. Amazing value for under $30. Very well thought out kit. Ideal for hikes and camping or anyplace else you may want a light weight but fully functional wound kit.

This is a fantastic addition to a household first aid kits. It addresses moderate lacerations, which most kits aren't very equipped for. Fantastic if you are out and about and may not have immediate access to suturing. Got everything you need if you are dealing with a decent sized cut. There were a couple of times where I could really have used this kit in the past. When I didn't, the wound resulted in some problems and eventual scarring.

The SurviveX Zip Stitch Wound Closure Strips offer a practical solution for treating minor wounds without the need for traditional stitches. This laceration closure kit is easy to use and provides a reliable way to close cuts and lacerations at home or while on-the-go. The kit includes clear instructions, making it accessible for anyone, even those without medical training. The strips adhere well to the skin and create a secure closure that promotes proper healing.

We tried these sutures out on "mock skin" used for surgical practice and found they work better than any of the other quick sutures we've tried - they go on easily and stick well, and pulling the "zips" closes them effectively. We suggest applying something like a Tegaderm patch overtop to keep the wound sterile and visible. Top marks, easy win and recommended for comprehensive first aid-kits.

This wound closure kit is a must for any serious first aid kit. It contains everything you need to deal with a long, deep cut, all contained in a compact, waterproof, sealed ziplock bag. Everything is clearly identified, and the kit comes with a helpful instruction card. The materials included are individually wrapped, sterile and of excellent quality. The wound closure strips are extremely effective if you follow the instructions, and the kit gives you everything you need to keep the wound clean and sterile until you can make it to an emergency room.

Perfect addition to a first aid kit in a heavy truck shop, and to have on hand for kids doing activities like hockey, soccer, extremely icy winter tubing, and hard core back yard water gun fights/obstacle courses! Very easy to apply and synch tight. Strong hold even with sweaty skin and oils. These feel like paramedic field-grade strips. Definitely a must-have if you are on any backwoods trips, impact sports, hunting, etc.
